name: FortiManager
description: This app performs firewall configuration and security policy management actions in FortiManager

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: Level type at which to create the firewall policy. Choosing 'ADOM' (Administrative Domain Name) of the FortiManager server allows you to perform against that particular ADOM

parameters.name: adom
parameters.description: ADOM name

parameters.name: package
parameters.description: The Policy Package name or full folder path to create the firewall policy within

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Policy name to create

parameters.name: source_interface
parameters.description: Incoming (ingress) interface to specify for the firewall policy. Interfaces must already exist in the FortiManager server. If specifying more than one, enter as a comma-separated list

parameters.name: destination_interface
parameters.description: Outgoing (egress) interface to specify for the firewall policy. Interfaces must already exist in the FortiManager server. If specifying more than one, enter as a comma-separated list

parameters.name: source_address
parameters.description: Source IPv4 Addresses, address objects, and/or address group names to specify for the firewall policy. If providing new address objects that are NOT of type Subnet (IPv4/Netmask format) or any new address groups that do not already exist in the FortiManager server, the action will fail. If specifying more than one, enter as a comma-separated list

parameters.name: destination_address
parameters.description: Destination IPv4 Addresses, address objects, and/or address group names to specify for the firewall policy. If providing new address objects that are NOT of type Subnet (IPv4/Netmask format) or any new address groups that do not already exist in the FortiManager server, the action will fail. If specifying more than one, enter as a comma-separated list

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Policy action to specify for the firewall policy. Accept: Allows sessions that match the firewall policy. Deny: Blocks sessions that match the firewall policy. IPSec: Firewall policy becomes a policy-based IPsec VPN policy

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: 'Enable' or 'Disable' this firewall policy on your FortiManager instance

parameters.name: schedule
parameters.description: Name for the schedule to be associated with the firewall policy (e.g. always, none)

parameters.name: service
parameters.description: Service and Service group names to create for the firewall policy. If specifying more than one, enter as a comma-separated list

parameters.name: inspection_mode
parameters.description: Firewall policy Inspection Mode

parameters.name: log_traffic
parameters.description: Enables or disables logging of either all sessions or only security profile sessions

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: Level type at which to list the firewall policies

parameters.name: adom
parameters.description: ADOM name. Only required for 'ADOM' Firewall Policies

parameters.name: package
parameters.description: Policy Package name

parameters.name: package_path
parameters.description: The full folder path nested within the policy package

parameters.name: policy_name
parameters.description: Specific firewall policy name whose details you want to retrieve

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: Level type at which to update the firewall policy. Choosing 'ADOM' (Administrative Domain Name) of the FortiManager server allows you to perform against that particular ADOM

parameters.name: adom
parameters.description: ADOM name

parameters.name: package
parameters.description: The Policy Package name or full folder path to update the firewall policy within

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The policy name that will be updated. Note: this does not update the policy name itself

parameters.name: source_interface
parameters.description: Incoming (ingress) interface to add to the firewall policy. Interfaces must already exist in the FortiManager server. If specifying more than one, enter as a comma-separated list

parameters.name: destination_interface
parameters.description: Outgoing (egress) interface to specify for the firewall policy. Interfaces must already exist in the FortiManager server. If specifying more than one, enter as a comma-separated list

parameters.name: source_address
parameters.description: Source IPv4 Addresses, address objects, and/or address group names to specify for the firewall policy. If specifying more than one, enter as a comma-separated list

parameters.name: destination_address
parameters.description: Destination IPv4 Addresses, address objects, and/or address group names to specify for the firewall policy. If specifying more than one, enter as a comma-separated list

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Policy action to specify for the firewall policy. Accept: Allows sessions that match the firewall policy. Deny: Blocks sessions that match the firewall policy. IPSec: Firewall policy becomes a policy-based IPsec VPN policy

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: 'Enable' or 'Disable' this firewall policy on your FortiManager instance

parameters.name: schedule
parameters.description: Name for the schedule to be associated with the firewall policy (e.g. always, none)

parameters.name: service
parameters.description: Service and Service group names to create for the firewall policy. If specifying more than one, enter as a comma-separated list

parameters.name: inspection_mode
parameters.description: Firewall policy Inspection Mode

parameters.name: log_traffic
parameters.description: Enables or disables logging of either all sessions or only security profile sessions

actions.name: Install Firewall Policy
actions.description: Install a firewall policy on a device

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: Level Type

parameters.name: adom
parameters.description: Administrative Domain Name

parameters.name: generate_rev
parameters.description: Generate new ADOM revision before install

parameters.name: adom_rev_comments
parameters.description: If 'generate_rev' flag is set, the comment for the new ADOM revision

parameters.name: adom_rev_name
parameters.description: If 'generate_rev' flag is set, the name for the new ADOM revision

parameters.name: dev_rev_comments
parameters.description: Comments for the device configuration revision that will be generated during install

parameters.name: policy_pkg
parameters.description: Source package path and name

parameters.name: scope_name
parameters.description: Scope name

parameters.name: scope_vdom
parameters.description: Scope VDOM

parameters.name: preview
parameters.description: Generate preview cache only

parameters.name: unassign
parameters.description: Remove global policy from ADOM

parameters.name: ifpolicy_only
parameters.description: Only install interface policies

parameters.name: no_ifpolicy
parameters.description: Install regular policies only - do not install interface policies

parameters.name: objs_only
parameters.description: Install objects only - do not install any policies

parameters.name: auto_lock_ws
parameters.description: Automatically lock and unlock workspace when performing security console task

parameters.name: copy_only
parameters.description: Only copy to device db

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: Level Type

parameters.name: adom
parameters.description: Administrative Domain Name

parameters.name: address_type
parameters.description: Type of address to create

parameters.name: address_name
parameters.description: Address name

parameters.name: ip_netmask
parameters.description: IP address or IP address and netmask. Examples of valid formats: 1.1.1.1, 1.1.1.1/32, 1.1.1.1/255.255.255.255

parameters.name: fqdn
parameters.description: Fully Qualified Domain Name

parameters.name: policy_group_name
parameters.description: Name of policy group to be added to address

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: Level type

parameters.name: adom
parameters.description: ADOM name

parameters.name: address_name
parameters.description: Name of address object to delete

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: Level type

parameters.name: adom
parameters.description: ADOM name

parameters.name: address_name
parameters.description: Name of address object to retrieve. If none is specified, this action will return all matching values.

parameters.name: filter_by
parameters.description: Criteria to filter results by. Use the following format to specify filter: [["{attribute}", "==", "{value}"]]

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of addresses to return. Default is 0, which returns all results.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The starting point of the results to be returned.

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: Level type

parameters.name: adom
parameters.description: ADOM name

parameters.name: address_name
parameters.description: Name of address object to update

parameters.name: ip_netmask
parameters.description: Updated IP address and netmask (e.g. 0.0.0.0/32) to assign address object

parameters.name: fqdn
parameters.description: Updated Fully Qualified Domain Name to assign address object

parameters.name: policy_group_name
parameters.description: Name of policy group to be added to address

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: Level type at which to block IP addresses

parameters.name: adom
parameters.description: ADOM name. Only required for 'ADOM' blocking of IP addresses

parameters.name: package
parameters.description: Policy package name

parameters.name: package_path
parameters.description: The full folder path nested within the policy package

parameters.name: policy_name
parameters.description: Specific firewall policy name for blocking IP addresses

parameters.name: address_group_name
parameters.description: Specific address group name for for blocking IP addresses

parameters.name: ip_addresses
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of IP addresses to block

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: Level type at which to unblock IP addresses

parameters.name: adom
parameters.description: ADOM name. Only required for 'ADOM' unblocking of IP addresses

parameters.name: package
parameters.description: Policy package name

parameters.name: package_path
parameters.description: The full folder path nested within the policy package

parameters.name: policy_name
parameters.description: Specific firewall policy name for unblocking IP addresses

parameters.name: address_group_name
parameters.description: Specific address group name for for unblocking IP addresses

parameters.name: ip_addresses
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of IP addresses to unblock

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: Level type at which to create the firewall policy. Choosing 'ADOM' (Administrative Domain Name) of the FortiManager server allows you to perform against that particular ADOM

parameters.name: adom
parameters.description: ADOM name

parameters.name: package
parameters.description: The Policy Package name or full folder path of the firewall policy to delete

parameters.name: policy_id
parameters.description: Policy ID (can be retrieved from 'List Firewall Policies' action) to delete

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: Level type at which to block URLs. Choosing 'ADOM' (Administrative Domain Name) of the FortiManager server allows you to perform against that particular ADOM

parameters.name: adom
parameters.description: ADOM name

parameters.name: web_filter_profile_name
parameters.description: The Web Filter profile name to use to block URLs

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: URL to block

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Type of URL format. Wildcard must include a '*'

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: Level type at which to unblock URLs. Choosing 'ADOM' (Administrative Domain Name) of the FortiManager server allows you to perform against that particular ADOM

parameters.name: adom
parameters.description: ADOM name

parameters.name: web_filter_profile_name
parameters.description: The Web Filter profile name to use to unblock URLs

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: URL to unblock

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: Level type at which to create the address group. Choosing 'ADOM' (Administrative Domain Name) of the FortiManager server allows you to perform the action against that particular ADOM

parameters.name: adom
parameters.description: ADOM name

parameters.name: address_group_name
parameters.description: Unique name of address group you want to create

parameters.name: members
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of address objects or address groups to add to the address group. Accepted values include FortiManager address object names, IP/netmask, and FQDN

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: Level type at which to create the address group. Choosing 'ADOM' (Administrative Domain Name) of the FortiManager server allows you to perform the action against that particular ADOM

parameters.name: adom
parameters.description: ADOM name

parameters.name: address_group_name
parameters.description: Unique name of address group you want to create
